channel.title: Home on just a note
channel.description: Recent content in Home on just a note

item.title: Basic KaTeX Math Guide
item.description: Basic KaTeX Math Guide Add math: true to your front matter to enable math. Inline Math Wrap with $: $E = mc^2$ $a^2 + b^2 = c^2$ $\frac{1}{2}$ Display Math Wrap with $$: $$ \frac{-b \pm \sqrt{b^2 - 4ac}}{2a} $$ Fractions $\frac{1}{2}$ inline $$ \frac{n!}{k!(n-k)!} $$ Exponents $x^2$ and $x^{2n}$ $$ e^{i\pi} + 1 = 0 $$ Subscripts $x_1$ and $x_{ij}$ $$ \sum_{i=1}^{n} i = \frac{n(n+1)}{2} $$ Roots $\sqrt{x}$ and $\sqrt[3]{x}$

item.title: Archimedes: The Greatest Mathematician of Antiquity
item.description: Archimedes Genius of Ancient Greece Who Was Archimedes? Mathematician, physicist, engineer (287-212 BC) From Syracuse, Sicily One of the greatest minds in history The Eureka Moment While taking a bath, Archimedes discovered the principle of buoyancy and ran through the streets shouting: "Eureka! Eureka!" Key Contributions Mathematics Value of π (pi) Volume of sphere Method of exhaustion Physics Buoyancy principle Law of the lever Center of gravity Archimedes' Screw An ingenious device for raising water:

item.title: Describing Data
item.description: (copied from “MathinSociety” Book) Measures of Central Tendency Let’s begin by trying to find the most “typical” value of a data set. Note that we just used the word “typical” although in many cases you might think of using the word “average.” We need to be careful with the word “average” as it means different things to different people in different contexts. One of the most common uses of the word “average” is what mathematicians and statisticians call the arithmetic mean, or just plain old mean for short.

item.title: Jp Learn Day 1
item.description: Japanese Language Course: Lesson 1 - Daily Vocabulary 1. Greetings Japanese Romaji Meaning Example おはよう Ohayou Good morning おはよう、元気？ (Good morning, you okay?) こんにちは Konnichiwa Hello こんにちは！ (Hello!) こんばんは Konbanwa Good evening こんばんは！ (Good evening!) おやすみ Oyasumi Good night おやすみ！ (Good night!) じゃあね Jaa ne Bye じゃあね！ (See you!) Note: Add ございます (gozaimasu) to おはよう/こんにちは for politeness. 2. Basic Expressions Japanese Romaji Meaning Example ありがとう Arigatou Thank you ありがとう！ (Thanks!) すみません Sumimasen Excuse me / Sorry すみません！ (Excuse me!

item.title: Bangun Datar
item.description: Here is a LaTeX circle example: $$\bigcirc$$ He!!!

item.title: Perbandingan
item.description: Perbandingan senilai & berbalik nilai Perbandingan senilai $$ \frac{a_{1}}{b_{1}} = \frac{a_{2}}{b_{2}} $$ Di sebuah depot air, 3 galon air dapat diisi dalam waktu 10 menit. Berapa galon air yang dapat diisi dalam waktu 1 jam? Di pasar, harga 5 kg beras adalah Rp 60.000. Jika Bu Siti ingin membeli 12 kg beras, berapa total harga yang harus dia bayar? Jika 3 liter susu cukup untuk 12 gelas, berapa liter susu yang dibutuhkan untuk 30 gelas?

item.title: Calculus Fundamentals
item.description: Differentiation Power Rule The derivative of $x^n$ is: $$ \frac{d}{dx} x^n = nx^{n-1} $$ Example: $$ \frac{d}{dx} x^3 = 3x^2 $$ Chain Rule For composite functions: $$ \frac{d}{dx} f(g(x)) = f’(g(x)) \cdot g’(x) $$ Integration Basic Integral The antiderivative of $x^n$ (for $n \neq -1$): $$ \int x^n , dx = \frac{x^{n+1}}{n+1} + C $$ Example: $$ \int x^2 , dx = \frac{x^3}{3} + C $$ Definite Integral Area under curve from $a$ to $b$: $$ \int_a^b f(x) , dx = F(b) - F(a) $$ where $F’(x) = f(x)$.

item.title: Coba2
item.description: 1. Scrollable List Top 50 Programming Languages Python JavaScript Java TypeScript C++ C# PHP Swift Kotlin Ruby Go Rust Scala Dart Perl Lua R MATLAB Haskell Elixir Clojure Groovy F# Julia Crystal Nim VBA ABAP COBOL Fortran Lisp Prolog Erlang OCaml Scheme Ada Pascal Delphi PL/SQL T-SQL Bash PowerShell VHDL Verilog Assembly C Zig V Odin Carbon Derivation of the Quadratic Formula Start with the standard form: $$ax^2 + bx + c = 0$$

item.title: Setting up zram in Artix Linux
item.description: Here is the step for configuring zram in Artix Linux Create /etc/modules-load.d/zram.conf zram Create /etc/modprobe.d/zram.conf options zram num_devices=1 Create /etc/udev/rules.d/10-zram.rules bash ACTION=="add", KERNEL=="zram0", ATTR{initstate}=="0", ATTR{comp_algorithm}="zstd", ATTR{disksize}="4G", RUN="/usr/bin/mkswap -U clear %N" Finally, add zram to fstab for automount Edit /etc/fstab /dev/zram0 none swap defaults,discard,pri=100 0 0 Reboot to take the effect immediately After reboot, Check output with zramctl Voila!! Reference link : https://wiki.archlinux.org/title/ZRAM https://wiki.gentoo.org/wiki/Zram

item.title: My NixOS installation notes
item.description: Just some notes :D Lets go to the plan for the whole system structure :D Partition Filesystem Mountpoint Subvolume Compress (level) /dev/sda1 FAT32 /boot/efi - - /dev/sda2 btrfs / root, home zstd (1:5) Create root partition mkfs.btrfs /dev/sda2 Mount the root partition mount /dev/sda2 /mnt Create btrfs subvolume ( home and root) btrfs create subvolume /mnt/home btrfs create subvolume /mnt/root umount /mnt Create mountpoint and mount the partition respectively mkdir /mnt/boot mkdir /mnt/home mount -o compress=zstd,subvol=root /dev/sda2 /mnt mount -o compress=zstd,subvol=home /dev/sda2 /mnt/home mount /dev/sda1 /mnt/boot Generate the config and copy it to the root partition

item.title: Eid Mubarak 2025
item.description: Eid Mubarak 1446H As the crescent moon is sighted and the holy month of Ramadan concludes, we celebrate Eid al-Fitr 1446 H with joy and gratitude. May this Eid bring you and your family: Peace like the serenity of a mosque Strength like the trunk of a palm Sweetness like dates from its branches Growth like its ever-reaching fronds Eid Mubarak from our family to yours!

item.title: Add a Katex to Hugo Website
item.description: If you’re downloading manually: Go to KaTeX GitHub Releases Download the latest katex.tar.gz or katex.zip Extract all and copy to static folder (see below!) static/ └── katex/ └── katex.min.css ├── katex.min.js └── contrib/ ← Essential folder name └── auto-render.min.js Once your files are in the right place, your layouts/partials/katex.html should reference them with the correct paths: css <!-- KaTeX CSS --> <link rel="stylesheet" href="/katex/katex.min.css"> <!-- KaTeX core JS --> <script defer src="/katex/katex.

item.title: Hugo Post Format
item.description: All list of custom shortcodes used in my Hugo theme :) No Shortcode Meaning Option 1 scroll-vertical Adding vertical scrollable to any content height = <insert_number>px 2 scrollable Adding horizontal scrollable to any content none 3 code Adding coding format in each language language, filename, location 4

item.title: Hugo Website Manual Deployment to SourceHut Pages
item.description: Here is my script used in Hugo Website deployment to SourceHut Pages Be sure to check the configuration format or file location correctly! bash #!/bin/bash # Simple Hugo to SourceHut Pages Deploy Script # Deploys from ~/websites/blog to SourceHut Pages # Configuration SITE_NAME="mhabib12345" # Your site name (without .srht.site) HUGO_DIR="$HOME/blog" # Hugo project directory PUBLIC_DIR="public" # Hugo output directory TARBALL="/tmp/site.tar.gz" # Temporary tarball path # Colors for output RED='\033[0;31m' GREEN='\033[0;32m' YELLOW='\033[1;33m' NC='\033[0m' # No Color # Check for required commands check_dependency() { if !

item.title: Math Test
item.description: Inline Math This is inline math: $E = mc^2$ Display Math $$ \frac{-b \pm \sqrt{b^2 - 4ac}}{2a} $$ More Complex $$ \int_{-\infty}^{\infty} e^{-x^2} dx = \sqrt{\pi} $$
